task: I need to document a near-miss at work and send a follow-up notice to the people involved. Can you help me put together a short draft that records what happened, what action is required, and any next steps in a straightforward way?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, straightforward draft you can use and edit:

---

**Subject:** Near-Miss Notification and Follow-Up Action

Team,

This is to document a near-miss that occurred on **[date/time]** at **[location]**.

**What happened:**  
Briefly describe the event, including what was happening, who was involved, and how the near-miss was identified. No injury/damage occurred, but the situation had the potential to cause harm.

**Immediate action taken:**  
State what was done right away to make the area safe and prevent recurrence.

**Required follow-up:**  
- Review the incident and identify the cause  
- Confirm any corrective actions needed  
- Complete any required inspection, repair, or procedure update  
- Report back by **[deadline]**

**Next steps:**  
Please acknowledge receipt of this notice and confirm who is responsible for each follow-up item. If additional information is needed, let me know.

Thanks,  
**[Your name]**  
**[Title / Contact info]**

---

If you want, I can also turn this into:
1. a more formal incident report style, or  
2. a tighter message you can send by email or Teams.

user_simulator.text: Too generic. I need something I can actually send without babysitting it. Give me a draft that already has the incident language, action required, and follow-up baked in — not placeholders and options. Keep it tight, direct, and formal enough to hold up if it gets read later.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a tighter draft you can send as-is and edit only for the facts:

---

**Subject:** Near-Miss Notification and Required Follow-Up

Team,

This notice records a near-miss that occurred on **[date/time]** at **[location]**. During the work, a condition developed that had the potential to cause injury and/or damage, but no injury, damage, or release occurred.

The near-miss is being documented so the event can be reviewed, the cause confirmed, and any corrective action assigned before the work continues or is repeated.

**Required action:**  
The involved parties are to review the event, confirm the cause, identify any corrective measures needed, and complete the required follow-up actions. Any immediate controls needed to prevent recurrence must be put in place before further work proceeds.

**Next steps:**  
Please acknowledge receipt of this notice, confirm who is responsible for the follow-up items, and provide a status update by **[deadline]**. If additional information is needed to complete the review, advise as soon as possible.

Regards,  
**[Your name]**  
**[Title / Contact info]**

---

If you want, I can make it even more site-ready and sharper in tone, or turn it into a version that reads more like a formal incident notice.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still too soft in a couple spots. I need it to read like a real notice, not a template pretending to be one.

Give me a version that already says the event was a near-miss, the work is to be reviewed, the correction is required, and the recipients must confirm responsible parties and closeout. Tight. No filler lines like “if additional information is needed.” Just the notice.

turns.agent_response:
Certainly — here’s a tighter, more formal notice you can send:

---

**Subject:** Near-Miss Notice and Required Follow-Up

Team,

This notice records a near-miss that occurred on **[date/time]** at **[location]**. The event did not result in injury or damage, but it did present a credible risk of harm and must be treated as a reportable safety issue.

The work is to be reviewed to confirm what occurred, identify the cause, and determine the correction required before the task is continued or repeated. Any immediate controls needed to prevent recurrence are to be implemented without delay.

The recipients of this notice are to confirm the responsible parties for the review, correction, and closeout, and provide the agreed follow-up status by **[deadline]**.

Regards,  
**[Your name]**  
**[Title / Contact info]**

---

If you want, I can make it even more site-hard, with a firmer tone and less “notice” language.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but still too polite in the wrong places. I need it to read like an actual notice I’d send out of the trailer, not a cleaned-up memo.

Give me a version that is firmer on the required actions and uses cleaner compliance language — something like: the event was a near-miss, the work shall be reviewed, the correction shall be completed, and the responsible parties shall be confirmed with closeout due by the deadline. No softeners, no offer to help, no extra commentary.

turns.agent_response:
**Subject:** Near-Miss Notice and Required Closeout

Team,

This notice records a near-miss that occurred on **[date/time]** at **[location]**. No injury or damage resulted, but the event presented a credible hazard and shall be treated as a safety issue requiring review.

The work shall be reviewed to confirm what occurred and determine the cause. The required correction shall be completed before the task is continued or repeated. Any immediate controls needed to prevent recurrence shall be implemented without delay.

The responsible parties for the review, correction, and closeout shall be confirmed, and closeout shall be submitted by **[deadline]**.

**[Your name]**  
**[Title / Contact info]**
